texto: My eyes open and close. I catch a quick glimpse of the people in the room and hear a fading echo from the heart monitor. As I think about my past, remorse bleed into the crevices of my torn heart. Since I’m in this hospital, I might as well prepare for my departure. I like the way that word sounds, it gives off the illusion of a specific journey that an individual is about to take.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a T.K. Ware: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “My eyes open and close. I catch a quick glimpse of the people in the room and hear a fading echo …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, young-adult-fiction, heart pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e reflexao.

frase_original: My eyes open and close. I catch a quick glimpse of the people in the room and hear a fading echo from the heart monitor. As I think about my past, remorse bleed into the crevices of my torn heart. Since I’m in this hospital, I might as well prepare for my departure. I like the way that word sounds, it gives off the illusion of a specific journey that an individual is about to take.

texto: In some cases, the desires of your flesh are stronger than your spirit, and you revisit the temptation that you once repented of. Instead of asking only for forgiveness, you must also ask for the strength to withstand the temptation. There is strength available for any situation we are going through. All you have to do is simply ask from a sincere heart. God knows if you are serious and He knows when you are simply babbling
explicacao: Orientação direta de T.K. Ware sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “In some cases, the desires of your flesh are stronger than your spirit, and you revisit the tempt…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, inspirational-living, reflexao, útil em momentos de inspiracao e reflexao.
